figures.name: Roger Williams
figures.realBlurb: Banished from Massachusetts Bay in 1636, he founded Providence on land bought from the Narragansett and championed soul-liberty and separation of church and state.

gag.destination: Narragansett Bay canoe launch, Seekonk side
gag.description: Booted out of Salem mid-winter — needs a lift to the riverbank to start a whole new colony before the snow sets in. Will pay in religious tolerance.

figures.name: Anne Hutchinson
figures.realBlurb: Antinomian dissenter exiled from Massachusetts in 1638; helped settle Portsmouth on Aquidneck Island after holding theological meetings in her home.

gag.description: Needs folding chairs delivered for a women's theology meeting the magistrates definitely won't approve of. Discretion appreciated.

figures.name: Nathanael Greene
figures.realBlurb: Quaker-raised Coventry foundryman who became Washington's most trusted general and wore down Cornwallis in the brutal Southern Campaign.

gag.serviceName: Ride to the foundry (and back, slowly)
gag.description: Walks with a limp and got kicked out of Quaker meeting for soldiering — needs a patient driver who won't mind a strategic retreat or three on the way home.

figures.name: Gilbert Stuart
figures.realBlurb: Saunderstown-born portraitist whose unfinished 1796 'Athenaeum' portrait of Washington became the face on the one-dollar bill; his father ran America's first snuff mill.

gag.description: Out of Naples yellow with Washington sitting for him in an hour. Also grab snuff. Warning: he will leave the portrait unfinished anyway.

figures.name: Oliver Hazard Perry
figures.realBlurb: South Kingstown naval hero of the 1813 Battle of Lake Erie who reported victory with 'We have met the enemy and they are ours.'

gag.description: His flagship got shot to splinters mid-battle, so he rowed to a backup ship. Needs a lift to the second boat. Bring oars and a flag reading DON'T GIVE UP THE SHIP.

figures.name: Matthew C. Perry
figures.realBlurb: Newport-born commodore (Oliver's younger brother) who sailed 'Black Ships' into Tokyo Bay in 1853–54 and forced Japan to open to Western trade.

gag.description: Needs help knocking on a country's door that has been politely closed for 200 years. Will not take no for an answer. Steam-powered ride preferred.

figures.name: Ambrose Burnside
figures.realBlurb: Bristol firearms inventor, three-time RI governor, first NRA president, and Civil War general whose facial hair gave us the word 'sideburns.'

gag.description: The sideburns won't trim themselves and Fredericksburg didn't go great. Needs a steady hand and someone to tell him the assault was a bad idea.

figures.name: Esek Hopkins
figures.realBlurb: Scituate-born first Commander-in-Chief of the Continental Navy (1775); his raid on Nassau was the new navy's first amphibious operation.

gag.description: Got the entire brand-new American navy — all eight ships of it — and nowhere to park. Carpool the crew? Court-martial pending, so hurry.

figures.name: Thomas Tew
figures.realBlurb: Newport 'gentleman pirate' of the 1690s whose Red Sea raids made him fabulously rich; he was killed in 1695 when a cannon shot disemboweled him.

gag.description: Needs a same-day cannonballectomy and a second opinion. Tip jar overflowing with Mughal gold. Please don't ask where it came from.

figures.name: Ida Lewis
figures.realBlurb: Keeper of Newport's Lime Rock Light who rescued at least 18 people (locals say up to 36); the lighthouse was renamed in her honor — the only such case in the U.S.

gag.serviceName: Late ride to the harbor (someone's drowning)
gag.description: Two soldiers in the water again. She'll row; just needs a ride to her boat 'Rescue' and someone to hold her lamp. The 'Grace Darling of America' does not do dry feet.

figures.name: Metacomet
figures.realBlurb: Wampanoag sachem, known to the English as King Philip, who led the devastating 1675–76 war against the New England colonies; he was killed near Mount Hope in Bristol.

gag.description: Diplomatic talks have completely broken down. Needs a discreet ride back to the home base before the colonial militias circle the swamp. No questions.

figures.name: Sullivan Ballou
figures.realBlurb: Smithfield-born major in the 2nd RI Infantry whose tender July 1861 letter to his wife Sarah ('my love for you is deathless') became famous after Bull Run, where he was mortally wounded.

gag.destination: Post office, then Bull Run, Virginia
gag.description: Has one last letter for Sarah and a bad feeling about the morning. Needs it delivered no matter what. Bring tissues — the driver will cry.

figures.name: Stephen Hopkins
figures.realBlurb: Ten-term RI governor and Declaration signer who quipped 'My hand trembles, but my heart does not' as he signed with a palsied hand.

gag.description: His hand shakes too much to drive himself to commit treason against the Crown. Heart's steady, though. Bring a steady pen.

figures.name: William Blackstone
figures.realBlurb: Eccentric early settler (name also spelled 'Blaxton') who planted New England's first apple orchard and reportedly rode a trained bull around the Cumberland/Lonsdale area.

gag.description: Usually commutes by trained white bull, but he's saddle-sore. Needs a lift to the orchard to hand out apples to passing strangers. Bull stays home.

figures.name: Samuel Slater
figures.realBlurb: 'Father of the American Industrial Revolution' who smuggled British textile-mill secrets in his head and built the first water-powered cotton mill in Pawtucket (1793).

gag.description: Memorized an entire factory in his head and crossed an ocean. Needs a ride to the falls and a friend who can keep an industrial secret from the British.

figures.name: Abraham Whipple
figures.realBlurb: Providence sea captain who in 1772 led the burning of the British revenue schooner Gaspee — arguably the first armed act of the Revolution.

gag.serviceName: Late-night ride to the sandbar (bring rowboats)
gag.description: A British customs boat has run aground and it would be a shame if something happened to it. Muffled oars only. Marshmallows optional.

figures.name: Elizabeth Buffum Chace
figures.realBlurb: Valley Falls Quaker abolitionist and suffragist who ran an Underground Railroad station and is sometimes called 'the conscience of Rhode Island.'

gag.destination: Valley Falls safe house, Central Falls
gag.description: Needs a quiet, reliable driver for guests passing through after dark on their way to freedom. The conscience of Rhode Island vouches for you.

figures.name: Thomas Wilson Dorr
figures.realBlurb: Led the 1842 Dorr Rebellion for voting rights; his cannons literally failed to fire at the Providence arsenal and his rebel stand fizzled on Acote's Hill in Chepachet.

gag.serviceName: Ride to the arsenal (and a gunsmith)
gag.destination: Providence Arsenal, then Acote's Hill, Chepachet
gag.description: Storming the arsenal for the right to vote, but his Revolutionary-War cannons keep misfiring. Needs a lift and someone who knows why old powder gets damp.

figures.name: George M. Cohan
figures.realBlurb: Providence-born 'Yankee Doodle Dandy' songwriter and showman who wrote 'Give My Regards to Broadway' and 'Over There'; claimed a Fourth of July birthday.

gag.serviceName: Ride to the train station (regards to deliver)
gag.description: Born on the Fourth of July (he insists), needs a ride to catch the New York train. Has many regards that absolutely must be given to Broadway in person.

figures.name: H.P. Lovecraft
figures.realBlurb: Providence horror writer (1890–1937) who set cosmic dread in New England, walked the city's old streets by night, and is buried in Swan Point Cemetery.

gag.description: Won't go out before dark, fears refrigeration and the ocean, and needs a 2 a.m. walking-tour ride past the Fleur-de-Lys building. Driver must not be eldritch. Probably.

figures.name: Edgar Allan Poe
figures.realBlurb: Courted Providence poet Sarah Helen Whitman in 1848, proposed to her in a cemetery, and lost her when he broke his sobriety pledge two days before the wedding.

gag.serviceName: Ride to the cemetery (it's a date)
gag.destination: Swan Point Cemetery, then the Athenaeum, Providence
gag.description: Has a proposal planned among the gravestones and a wedding in two days. Please confiscate any alcohol along the route. Nevermore late, ideally.

figures.name: Sarah Helen Whitman
figures.realBlurb: Providence Transcendentalist poet, spiritualist, and Poe's fiancée who dumped him over a broken sobriety pledge and later defended his reputation for life.

gag.description: Needs a quiet lift away from the Athenaeum after calling off the wedding. Bring her handkerchief and ether. Will write a defense of him later anyway.

figures.name: Spalding Gray
figures.realBlurb: Barrington-born monologuist of 'Swimming to Cambodia,' famous for performing seated at a plain table; he died by suicide in NY Harbor in 2004.

gag.serviceName: Errand: move one table and a glass of water
gag.description: Needs his plain wooden table and notebook hauled to the theater for tonight's monologue. That's the whole set. He'll talk; you carry.

figures.name: David Byrne
figures.realBlurb: Talking Heads frontman who met his bandmates at RISD in Providence in the early '70s; the band famously formed out of the art-school scene.

gag.serviceName: Ride to RISD (with an oversized item)
gag.destination: Rhode Island School of Design, Providence
gag.description: Needs help loading an enormous gray suit and his bicycle for a gig. May ask, en route, how he got here, and where that highway goes to. Same as it ever was.

figures.name: Cormac McCarthy
figures.realBlurb: Born in Providence in 1933 before his family moved to Tennessee; the bleak Western novelist of 'Blood Meridian' and 'The Road' started life an Ocean Stater.

gag.destination: Anywhere bleak and far from Providence
gag.description: Born here but won't say so warmly. Needs a long ride across desolate country. Will narrate the landscape unpunctuated. No quotation marks permitted in the car.

figures.name: Galway Kinnell
figures.realBlurb: Pawtucket-born Pulitzer-winning poet ('The Book of Nightmares'), known for tender, earthy verse and reading 'St. Francis and the Sow.'

gag.destination: A sow's pen, somewhere rural RI
gag.description: Needs to get to a pig to remind it of its own loveliness, in verse. Bring boots. The Pawtucket Pulitzer poet pays in blessings.

figures.name: The RISD Muralists
figures.realBlurb: RISD students have a long tradition of guerrilla art, murals, and 'unsanctioned' public installations across Providence's old industrial spaces.

gag.serviceName: Cargo ride (don't ask about the cinderblocks)
gag.description: Needs 200 pounds of art supplies and a ladder moved at odd hours for a project the building's owner has not been told about. Standard RISD stuff.

figures.name: Buddy Cianci
figures.realBlurb: Providence's longest-serving and twice-convicted mayor, who assaulted a man with an ashtray and a fireplace log, did federal time, and sold 'Mayor's Own Marinara Sauce.'

gag.serviceName: Ride to the press conference (case of marinara in trunk)
gag.description: Back from prison and running again. Needs a lift, a comb for the toupee, and trunk space for jars of his own marinara. Leave the ashtray at home.

figures.name: Lincoln Chafee
figures.realBlurb: Former Warwick blacksmith-turned-senator, governor, and briefly presidential candidate who once campaigned on adopting the metric system.

gag.serviceName: Ride to the forge, then the debate
gag.destination: Warwick smithy, then a TV studio
gag.description: Needs a lift to shoe a horse, then to a debate where he'll propose the metric system again. Distance measured in kilometers, naturally.

figures.name: Claiborne Pell
figures.realBlurb: Newport patrician senator behind the Pell Grant; a believer in psychic phenomena who also championed the arts and humanities endowments.

gag.destination: Financial aid office, then a parapsychology lecture
gag.description: Wants to hand out college grants to deserving students, then catch a talk on psychic spoon-bending. Patrician manners; will tip generously.

figures.name: Theodore Francis Green
figures.realBlurb: RI governor and senator who served in the U.S. Senate into his 90s, becoming one of the oldest senators in history; the Providence airport bears his name.

gag.destination: The airport that's named after him
gag.description: Still working at an age when most folks have retired twice. Needs a steady ride to catch his flight to Washington. Yes, the airport is literally named for him.

figures.name: Vincent 'Buddy' the Statehouse pigeon
figures.realBlurb: Rhode Island's gilded Independent Man statue tops the State House dome — struck by lightning many times and once removed for repairs after taking a bolt.

gag.serviceName: Ride down from a great height (lightning damage)
gag.description: The Independent Man took ANOTHER lightning strike and needs a lift to the gilder for re-gold-leafing. Bring a very tall ladder and a lightning rod.

figures.name: John O. Pastore
figures.realBlurb: First Italian-American U.S. governor and senator; his 1969 grilling of Mister Rogers helped save public-TV funding.

gag.serviceName: Ride to a Senate hearing (be nice)
gag.description: Gruff exterior, soft heart. Needs a ride to a budget hearing where a man in a cardigan is going to make him cry on the record. Bring a hankie.

figures.name: Mercy Brown
figures.realBlurb: Exeter teenager exhumed in 1892 during a 'vampire' panic; her heart was burned and fed to her dying brother as a tuberculosis cure. New England's last vampire scare.

gag.serviceName: Late-night ride to the Exeter cemetery (bring a shovel)
gag.destination: Chestnut Hill Baptist Church cemetery, Exeter
gag.description: Needs a discreet midnight lift to her own graveside. Bring a shovel, a lantern, and an open mind. The town's worried about consumption again.

figures.name: Mr. Potato Head
figures.realBlurb: Created by Pawtucket's Hasbro in 1952, the first toy advertised on TV; he became an unofficial RI tourism mascot with giant statues across the state.

gag.serviceName: Ride to the doctor (lost my ear again)
gag.description: Misplaced an ear, a mustache, and possibly the nose. Needs a lift to swap parts. Driver: please check the seat cushions before pulling away.

figures.name: Michael Townsend & the Secret Mall Apartment crew
figures.realBlurb: Eight artists who built and secretly lived in a hidden apartment inside the Providence Place Mall from 2003–2007, hauling in a couch, china cabinet, and ~5,000 lbs of cinderblock.

gag.serviceName: Cargo ride (5,000 lbs of cinderblock, discreet)
gag.description: Needs cinderblocks, a couch, and a china cabinet quietly moved into a 'forgotten' corner of the mall. Use the freight elevator. Tell no one. It's art.

figures.name: The Perron Family
figures.realBlurb: Family who moved into a 1736 Harrisville farmhouse in 1971 and reported hauntings that inspired 'The Conjuring'; the alleged spirit was wrongly pinned on Bathsheba Sherman.

gag.serviceName: Errand: the broom keeps moving itself
gag.description: Need a handyman who isn't afraid of cold spots, self-relocating brooms, or piles of dirt that reappear on a clean floor. Salt provided. Stay till sunup if you dare.

figures.name: Alan Shawn Feinstein
figures.realBlurb: Cranston philanthropist (1931–2024) famed for newspaper-ad and billboard giveaways, his gold jacket, and inspiring 600,000+ kids to do 'good deeds' as Feinstein Junior Scholars.

gag.serviceName: Ride to the billboard (and the bank)
gag.description: Wants to give away money to whoever does the most good deeds this week, and needs a lift to update the billboard. Wear sunglasses; the gold jacket is bright.

figures.name: The Awful Awful
figures.realBlurb: Rhode Island's beloved frozen drink — 'Awful Big, Awful Good' — sold at Newport Creamery, a fixture of local diner culture and 'cabinet' (milkshake) lore.

gag.description: Out-of-staters keep calling it a 'milkshake.' Needs three Awful Awfuls and coffee milk delivered, plus someone to gently correct the vocabulary. Awful big tip.

figures.name: The Independent Man
figures.realBlurb: The 11-foot gilded statue atop the State House dome, struck by lightning dozens of times; he's been shot at, repaired, and re-gilded over the decades.

gag.description: Took yet another lightning bolt to the gold leaf. Needs a careful ride down from the dome and back up. Spear stays with him; he's independent about it.

figures.name: Coffee Milk
figures.realBlurb: Coffee milk — milk with coffee syrup — was named Rhode Island's official state beverage in 1993 after a fierce legislative showdown with Del's Lemonade fans.

gag.description: The diner's out of coffee syrup and there's a line of regulars getting restless. Needs cases delivered before the breakfast rush. Do NOT bring Del's; politics.

figures.name: Del's Lemonade cart
figures.realBlurb: Frozen lemonade dynasty whose recipe traces to 1840s Italy; the green-and-yellow carts are an inescapable RI summer institution.

gag.description: Cart of frozen lemonade needs to reach the boardwalk before it slushes. Refrigerated vehicle a plus. Lost the 1993 state-drink vote and is still bitter (and lemony).

figures.name: Quahog
figures.realBlurb: The hard-shell clam ('quahog,' from Narragansett 'poquauhock') is RI's state shell and the heart of stuffies, chowder, and clam-cake culture.

gag.serviceName: Ride to the clam shack (in a bucket)
gag.description: A bucket of quahogs requests transport to become stuffies before the chowder cook-off. Red, white, or clear broth — driver must not take sides. Sandy seats likely.

figures.name: The Gaspee
figures.realBlurb: Each June, Pawtuxet Village holds Gaspee Days, culminating in the ceremonial burning of a mock British schooner to mark the 1772 raid.

gag.serviceName: Cargo ride (one mock British schooner, flammable)
gag.description: Need a wooden boat hauled to the cove for the annual ceremonial burning. Marshmallows in back. Fire marshal already notified. Colonial costume optional.

figures.name: Lizzie Borden
figures.realBlurb: Fall River, Massachusetts woman acquitted in 1893 of the hatchet murders of her father and stepmother; the case spawned a famous rhyme and an enduring true-crime legend.

gag.serviceName: Errand: hardware store run (just gardening, honest)
gag.description: Needs a ride to return a hatchet and pick up a much smaller, less alarming garden tool. Will offer you pears in the barn. Maybe decline the pears.

figures.name: Robert Goddard
figures.realBlurb: Worcester, Massachusetts physicist and 'father of modern rocketry' who launched the first liquid-fueled rocket in 1926; once mocked by the press, later vindicated by the space age.

gag.serviceName: Ride to the launch field (fragile cargo)
gag.destination: Aunt Effie's farm field, Auburn, MA
gag.description: Has a liquid-fueled rocket to test and a newspaper that doubts he understands physics. Needs careful transport and someone to ignore the skeptics. May go up 41 feet.

figures.name: Emily Dickinson
figures.realBlurb: Amherst, Massachusetts poet of reclusive habit who rarely left her home and conducted much of her social life through letters and a famously private upstairs room.

gag.serviceName: Errand: please leave it at the door (don't knock)
gag.description: Needs groceries left on the step — she'll lower a basket. Do not ring; do not loiter; do not expect to see her face. A note slipped under the door is plenty.

figures.name: Frederick Law Olmsted
figures.realBlurb: Landscape architect whose firm shaped Roger Williams Park in Providence and the broader 'Emerald' parks movement across New England.

gag.serviceName: Ride to survey the park (bring stakes)
gag.description: Wants to walk every winding path before redesigning the carriage roads. Needs a ride, survey stakes, and patience — he'll stop to admire every sightline.

figures.name: John Brown
figures.realBlurb: Wealthy Providence merchant, China-trade magnate, and Brown University benefactor whose fortune was entangled in the slave trade — a complicated founding figure.

gag.destination: Brown's wharf, then the East Side mansion, Providence
gag.description: Needs his counting-house ledgers and China-trade tea chests moved up the hill. The university would like a word about the ledgers' contents. Bring a strong back.

figures.name: Moses Brown
figures.realBlurb: John's Quaker brother, an abolitionist who freed his enslaved workers, founded a school, and bankrolled Samuel Slater's Pawtucket mill.

gag.serviceName: Ride to the meeting house, then the mill
gag.destination: Moses Brown School, then Slater Mill, Pawtucket
gag.description: Needs a quiet ride to Quaker meeting and then to check on the new water-powered mill he's funding. Will gently disagree with his brother the whole way.

figures.name: Sarah Doyle
figures.realBlurb: Providence educator and women's-rights advocate who helped found the women's college (later Pembroke) at Brown in the 1890s.

gag.description: Needs a lift to argue, again, that women should be allowed to study here. Bring petitions and patience. The future Pembroke College thanks you.

figures.name: Rocky Point Park
figures.realBlurb: Beloved Warwick amusement park (closed 1995) famous for its enormous Shore Dinner Hall serving clam cakes and chowder to thousands at once.

gag.destination: Rocky Point Shore Dinner Hall, Warwick
gag.description: Needs vats of chowder and mountains of clam cakes moved to the great hall before the crowd arrives. Nostalgia heavy; portions heavier. Watch the seagulls.

figures.name: George 'Cap' Wirth
figures.realBlurb: The 1895 Charles I. D. Looff carousel at Crescent Park in East Providence is a National Historic Landmark — a masterwork of hand-carved horses.

gag.destination: Crescent Park Looff Carousel, East Providence
gag.description: A prancing wooden horse needs gentle transport to the restorer and back to the spinning platform. Padding essential. Calliope music not included but encouraged.

figures.name: Doris Duke
figures.realBlurb: Tobacco heiress whose Newport mansion 'Rough Point' and her preservation of dozens of colonial buildings reshaped the city; an eccentric, fabulously wealthy recluse.

gag.description: Her pet camels, Princess and Baby, need transport along the cliffs. Also: please help move 80 colonial houses she's decided to save. The heiress tips in mansions.

figures.name: Bristol Fourth of July Committee
figures.realBlurb: Bristol, RI hosts the oldest continuous Fourth of July celebration in America (since 1785), complete with a red-white-and-blue center line painted down the parade route.

gag.description: Needs red, white, and blue paint hauled to repaint the center line before the nation's oldest July 4th parade. Be punctual; they've done this every year since 1785.

figures.name: William Coddington
figures.realBlurb: Founded Newport in 1639; later Quaker governor of the colony.

figures.name: Nicholas Easton
figures.realBlurb: Newport co-founder and colonial president; Easton's Beach bears his name.

figures.name: Dr. John Clarke
figures.realBlurb: Founded America's second Baptist church and drafted RI's 1663 charter.

gag.description: Needs to drop off a 56-page pamphlet titled 'Ill Newes from New-England.' Wait time included.

figures.name: Samuel Gorton
figures.realBlurb: Firebrand who founded Warwick after being jailed for calling magistrates 'Just Asses.'

gag.description: Please do not let him address the magistrates. He will call them 'Just Asses.' Again.

figures.name: Mary Dyer
figures.realBlurb: Quaker martyr who signed the Portsmouth Compact; hanged in Boston in 1660.

figures.name: William Dyer
figures.realBlurb: RI's first attorney general and Mary Dyer's husband.

figures.name: Obadiah Holmes
figures.realBlurb: Baptist preacher whipped 30 lashes in Boston for an unlicensed service.

figures.name: Joseph Bucklin
figures.realBlurb: Fired the shot that wounded the Gaspee's captain — 'the first blood of the Revolution.'

figures.name: Simeon Potter
figures.realBlurb: Bristol privateer and Gaspee raider with a dubious fortune.

figures.name: Nicholas Brown Jr.
figures.realBlurb: Merchant whose gift renamed the college 'Brown' in 1804.

figures.name: Canonicus
figures.realBlurb: Narragansett sachem who deeded Providence land to Roger Williams.

figures.name: Miantonomi
figures.realBlurb: Narragansett sachem who sold Aquidneck and Shawomet to the settlers.

figures.name: Ninigret
figures.realBlurb: Niantic sachem who navigated the colonial wars by staying neutral.

figures.name: Roger Mowry
figures.realBlurb: Kept Providence's first licensed tavern in 1655.

gag.description: Town meeting tonight; needs a barrel of flip and someone to keep the fire stoked.

figures.name: Benedict Arnold
figures.realBlurb: First governor under the 1663 charter (and the traitor's great-grandfather).

figures.name: William Brenton
figures.realBlurb: Colonial governor and vast landowner of Brenton's Neck.

figures.name: Gabriel Bernon
figures.realBlurb: Huguenot refugee who helped found Providence's first Anglican church.

figures.name: Pardon Tillinghast
figures.realBlurb: Baptist elder and merchant who built Providence's first wharf.

figures.name: Joseph Wanton
figures.realBlurb: Loyalist governor deposed in 1775 for refusing the revolution.

figures.name: William Barton
figures.realBlurb: Led the daring 1777 raid that captured British General Prescott from his bed.

gag.destination: a farmhouse on the West Road
gag.description: Pick up one (1) British general, still in his nightshirt. Quiet engine, please.

figures.name: Christopher Greene
figures.realBlurb: Colonel who led RI's Black Regiment at the Battle of Rhode Island.

figures.name: Silas Talbot
figures.realBlurb: Revolutionary naval officer and first captain of the USS Constitution.

figures.name: Stephen Olney
figures.realBlurb: Captain who stormed the redoubt at Yorktown.

figures.name: Zachariah Allen
figures.realBlurb: Inventor and mill owner who pioneered fire-prevention engineering.

figures.name: George Henry Corliss
figures.realBlurb: Built the colossal steam engine that powered the 1876 Centennial Exposition.

gag.description: One 1,400-horsepower steam engine, some assembly required. Do not lift with your back.

figures.name: Sissieretta Jones
figures.realBlurb: Providence soprano, the first Black artist to headline Carnegie Hall.

figures.name: Nelson W. Aldrich
figures.realBlurb: Powerful Gilded-Age senator, 'General Manager of the Nation.'

figures.name: John Chafee
figures.realBlurb: Marine, RI governor, senator, and Navy secretary.

figures.name: Bruce Sundlun
figures.realBlurb: Governor who took office during the 1991 credit-union collapse.

figures.name: Raymond L. S. Patriarca
figures.realBlurb: Ran New England's crime syndicate from a Federal Hill vending office for decades.

gag.description: Just a quiet errand to 'the office.' Don't write anything down. Don't make eye contact with the vending machines.

figures.name: Vincent 'Fat Vinny' Teresa
figures.realBlurb: Mobster-turned-informant who wrote a tell-all memoir.

figures.name: Joseph Bradford
figures.realBlurb: Newport merchant in the colonial triangle trade.

figures.name: William Ellery
figures.realBlurb: Newport lawyer and Declaration of Independence signer.

figures.name: Aaron Lopez
figures.realBlurb: Newport's wealthiest colonial merchant and a leader of Touro Synagogue.

figures.name: Henry Collins
figures.realBlurb: Newport merchant called the 'Lorenzo de' Medici of Rhode Island.'

figures.name: Sarah Kemble Knight
figures.realBlurb: Colonial diarist who recorded a famous 1704 horseback journey.

figures.name: Jemima Wilkinson
figures.realBlurb: Cumberland preacher who, after a fever, declared herself the 'Public Universal Friend.'

gag.description: Use no gendered pronouns; the rider is the Public Universal Friend. Genuinely ahead of their time.

figures.name: Ann Smith Franklin
figures.realBlurb: First woman to run a newspaper in America (Ben Franklin's sister-in-law).

figures.name: George Washington Greene
figures.realBlurb: Historian and grandson of General Nathanael Greene.

figures.name: Prudence Crandall
figures.realBlurb: Teacher who opened New England's first school for Black girls.

figures.name: Wilfred Harold 'Lefty' Flynn
figures.realBlurb: Bootleg-era Narragansett Bay rum-runner of local legend.

gag.serviceName: Ride to the dock, no headlights
gag.description: Cargo is 'Canadian.' If the Coast Guard asks, we are night fishing for tautog.

figures.name: Paul Cuffe
figures.realBlurb: Black Quaker sea captain and entrepreneur from just over the Massachusetts line.

figures.name: Oliver Hazard Perry Jr.
figures.realBlurb: Naval namesake descendant active in Newport maritime circles.

figures.name: Stephen Decatur Sr.
figures.realBlurb: Revolutionary privateer whose son became a naval legend.

figures.name: Welcome Arnold
figures.realBlurb: Providence merchant and Revolutionary-era assemblyman.

figures.name: John Howland
figures.realBlurb: Barber and reformer who championed Rhode Island's free public schools.

gag.serviceName: Ride to the barbershop, then the statehouse
gag.description: Will cut your hair AND lobby for public education in the same appointment. Efficient.

figures.name: Sullivan Dorr
figures.realBlurb: China-trade merchant whose Providence mansion still stands.

figures.name: Tristam Burges
figures.realBlurb: Congressman nicknamed the 'Bald Eagle of Rhode Island' for his oratory.

figures.name: James Mitchell Varnum
figures.realBlurb: Revolutionary general and lawyer who argued an early judicial-review case.

figures.name: Job Durfee
figures.realBlurb: Chief justice who presided over the Dorr treason trial.

figures.name: Wilkins Updike
figures.realBlurb: South County lawyer and antiquarian who chronicled the Narragansett planters.

figures.name: Thomas Poynton Ives
figures.realBlurb: Brown & Ives partner who built Providence's mercantile fortune.

figures.name: Sarah Updike Goddard
figures.realBlurb: Printer who ran Providence's first newspaper with her son.

figures.name: Esek Wilbur
figures.realBlurb: Quaker farmer-preacher of the South County plains.

figures.name: Caesar Lyndon
figures.realBlurb: Enslaved-then-free clerk to a colonial governor; kept a vivid Newport ledger.

figures.name: Newport Gardner
figures.realBlurb: Enslaved musician who bought his freedom and founded a Newport singing school.

figures.name: Duchess Quamino
figures.realBlurb: The 'Pastry Queen of Rhode Island,' a celebrated freed Newport baker.

gag.description: Three dozen frosted cakes for the Bellevue Ave set. Handle the boxes like they're made of spun sugar — they are.

figures.name: Cornelius Vanderbilt II
figures.realBlurb: Built The Breakers, the grandest of the Newport cottages.

figures.name: Alice Claypoole Vanderbilt
figures.realBlurb: 'Alice of the Breakers,' who ruled Newport society for 35 years.

gag.description: Needs a ride home between the third and fourth outfit of the day. Allow closet space in the car.

figures.name: Alva Vanderbilt Belmont
figures.realBlurb: Built Marble House, then became a militant suffrage leader.

gag.serviceName: Ride to Marble House, then the rally
gag.description: Drop the gowns, grab the 'Votes for Women' banner. She contains multitudes and will not be late to either.

figures.name: William Kissam Vanderbilt
figures.realBlurb: Railroad heir who gifted Marble House to Alva for her 39th birthday.

figures.name: Caroline Schermerhorn Astor
figures.realBlurb: 'The Mrs. Astor,' arbiter of the Gilded Age 'Four Hundred.'

gag.description: Park her directly in front of her own portrait. She prefers to be seen twice.

figures.name: John Jacob Astor IV
figures.realBlurb: Inventor, sci-fi novelist, and the richest man to die on the Titanic.

figures.name: Ward McAllister
figures.realBlurb: Social arbiter who coined 'the Four Hundred.'

figures.name: Harry Lehr
figures.realBlurb: 'King Lehr,' Newport's society jester who once threw a dinner for 100 dogs.

gag.description: Stewed liver and dog biscuits for 100 canine guests. The prince of Corsica (a monkey) eats first.

figures.name: Mamie Fish
figures.realBlurb: Caustic-witted hostess of the Triumvirate ('A toad, my pet, a toad').

figures.name: Tessie Oelrichs
figures.realBlurb: Comstock-silver heiress who built Rosecliff and threw the all-white Bal Blanc.

gag.description: White roses, white swans, and a fleet of mock white ships for the harbor. No color admitted, including the driver's car.

figures.name: Hermann Oelrichs
figures.realBlurb: Shipping magnate and swimmer who dared anyone to prove sharks attack.

gag.description: Wants to settle a $1,000 bet that no man with a rod and line can reel him in. Bring a stopwatch.

figures.name: Edward Berwind
figures.realBlurb: Coal baron who built The Elms with no backup electrical system.

figures.name: Julia Berwind
figures.realBlurb: Kept The Elms running Gilded-Age style into the 1960s; drove her own car, scandalously.

gag.description: She'll drive herself, thanks. Please inspect the steering wheel with white gloves on her return.

figures.name: George Peabody Wetmore
figures.realBlurb: RI governor and senator; his family kept Chateau-sur-Mer for a century.

figures.name: Oliver Hazard Perry Belmont
figures.realBlurb: Built Belcourt, whose entire first floor was stables for his beloved horses.

gag.description: Fifteen horses, each with a gold nameplate and pure-white monogrammed sheets. The horses ride; he'll walk.

figures.name: August Belmont
figures.realBlurb: Rothschild's American agent and namesake of the Belmont Stakes.

figures.name: Stanford White
figures.realBlurb: Architect of Rosecliff and the original Madison Square Garden.

figures.name: Richard Morris Hunt
figures.realBlurb: Architect of The Breakers, Marble House, and the Statue of Liberty base.

figures.name: Tina Weymouth
figures.realBlurb: Talking Heads bassist who met the band at RISD.

figures.name: Chris Frantz
figures.realBlurb: Talking Heads drummer and RISD alum.

figures.name: Seth MacFarlane
figures.realBlurb: RISD grad who created Family Guy and set it in Quahog, RI.

gag.description: Will voice half the passengers in the car. Pays in cutaway gags.

figures.name: Brian Chippendale
figures.realBlurb: Lightning Bolt drummer of the Fort Thunder Providence noise scene.

figures.name: Dale Chihuly
figures.realBlurb: Glass sculptor who studied and taught at RISD.

gag.description: One enormous hand-blown chandelier. Drive like every pothole is personal. It is.

figures.name: Chris Van Allsburg
figures.realBlurb: RISD-trained author of The Polar Express and Jumanji.

figures.name: Roz Chast
figures.realBlurb: New Yorker cartoonist and RISD graduate.

figures.name: Martin Mull
figures.realBlurb: Actor-comedian and RISD-trained painter.

figures.name: Nelson Eddy
figures.realBlurb: Providence-born singer and matinee idol of 1930s film operettas.

figures.name: Bobby Hackett
figures.realBlurb: Providence-born jazz cornetist.

figures.name: Eileen Farrell
figures.realBlurb: Woonsocket-born opera soprano with a jazz second act.

figures.name: Napoleon Lajoie
figures.realBlurb: Woonsocket-born Hall of Fame second baseman.

figures.name: Gabby Hartnett
figures.realBlurb: Woonsocket Hall of Fame catcher, 'Old Tomato Face.'

figures.name: Hugh Duffy
figures.realBlurb: Cranston-raised Hall of Famer who hit .440 in 1894.

figures.name: Davey Lopes
figures.realBlurb: Providence-born second baseman and base-stealing Dodger.

figures.name: Rocco Baldelli
figures.realBlurb: Woonsocket-born outfielder turned Twins manager.

figures.name: Vinny Paz
figures.realBlurb: Cranston boxer who came back to win titles after a broken neck.

gag.serviceName: Ride to the gym (cleared by the doctor)
gag.description: Yes, in the halo brace. No, he will not stop training. Pazmanian Devil rules apply.

figures.name: Ernie DiGregorio
figures.realBlurb: Providence College guard and NBA Rookie of the Year.

figures.name: Marvin Barnes
figures.realBlurb: Providence College star ('I'm not getting on no time machine').

gag.description: Refuses any flight that lands before it takes off. 'That's a time machine, baby.' Book a later one.

figures.name: Ernie Calverley
figures.realBlurb: URI legend famous for a 55-foot game-tying shot in 1946.

figures.name: Harold Stirling Vanderbilt
figures.realBlurb: Three-time America's Cup defender who also invented contract bridge.

gag.serviceName: Ride to the yacht, then the card table
gag.description: Defend the Cup by day, invent a card game by night. Bring a fresh deck and a stiff breeze.

figures.name: Brad Faxon
figures.realBlurb: Pro golfer raised in Barrington, RI.

figures.name: Ted Turner
figures.realBlurb: Won the 1977 America's Cup off Newport aboard Courageous.

figures.name: George Wein
figures.realBlurb: Founded the Newport Jazz and Folk Festivals.

gag.description: Dylan went electric here in '65. Bring earplugs and an open mind.

figures.name: Henry Hassenfeld
figures.realBlurb: Co-founded Hasbro (Hassenfeld Brothers) in Providence.

figures.name: Angelo DeLucia
figures.realBlurb: Brought the family's frozen-lemonade recipe from Naples to Cranston: Del's.

gag.description: Two large lemonades. Ask for a spoon and be judged for life. Squeeze, sip, repeat.

figures.name: Nibbles Woodaway
figures.realBlurb: The 58-foot Big Blue Bug over I-95, RI's most beloved termite.

gag.description: Nibbles needs a lift to the parade. He is 58 feet long and dressed for the holiday. Wide turns only.

figures.name: Claus von Bulow
figures.realBlurb: Tried (twice) over wife Sunny's coma at their Newport mansion Clarendon Court.

figures.name: Eileen Slocum
figures.realBlurb: Grande dame of 20th-century Newport society and GOP doyenne.

figures.name: Irene Castle
figures.realBlurb: Ballroom-dance superstar of the 1910s who summered in the area.

figures.name: Van Wickle
figures.realBlurb: Namesake of the Brown gates that open inward once a year.

figures.name: Theodore Francis Green Jr.
figures.realBlurb: Scion of the Green political line, active in Providence civic life.

figures.name: Rowland Hazard
figures.realBlurb: South County woolen magnate whose family seeded Peace Dale.

figures.name: Christiana Carteaux Bannister
figures.realBlurb: Narragansett-Black entrepreneur and patron; ran Boston salons, retired to Providence.

figures.name: Edward Mitchell Bannister
figures.realBlurb: Providence painter, a founder of the Providence Art Club.

figures.name: Gilbert Stuart Newton
figures.realBlurb: Painter and nephew of Gilbert Stuart.

figures.name: Paul Caranci
figures.realBlurb: North Providence historian and former deputy secretary of state.

figures.name: Matt Brown
figures.realBlurb: Young former RI secretary of state.

figures.name: Sheldon Whitehouse
figures.realBlurb: RI senator and former state attorney general.

figures.name: Jack Reed
figures.realBlurb: Senator and West Point graduate from Cranston.

figures.name: Bruce Sundlun Jr.
figures.realBlurb: Heir to the Sundlun civic name in Rhode Island politics.

figures.name: Vincent 'Buddy' Vallone
figures.realBlurb: Federal Hill restaurateur of local legend.

gag.description: Six quarts of gravy and a tray of braciole. Park by La Pigna arch; everyone will wave.

figures.name: Peter Griffin
figures.realBlurb: Hapless Quahog everyman and patriarch of the Griffin family.

gag.serviceName: Ride home from the Drunken Clam
gag.description: Cut him off two beers ago. Watch for the giant chicken; they have unfinished business.

figures.name: Lois Griffin
figures.realBlurb: Griffin matriarch and long-suffering piano teacher.

figures.name: Chris Griffin
figures.realBlurb: The Griffins' sweet, dim teenage son.

figures.name: Meg Griffin
figures.realBlurb: The perpetually put-upon Griffin daughter.

gag.description: Be nice to her. Genuinely. Nobody else is. 'Shut up, Meg' is not permitted in this vehicle.

figures.name: Stewie Griffin
figures.realBlurb: Football-headed Griffin baby and aspiring world-dominator.

gag.description: Cargo: one weather-control device. Do not ask. Do not touch the red button. Victory is mine.

figures.name: Brian Griffin
figures.realBlurb: The Griffins' talking, martini-drinking, novel-writing dog.

figures.name: Glenn Quagmire
figures.realBlurb: The Griffins' next-door neighbor and aviator. Giggity.

figures.name: Joe Swanson
figures.realBlurb: Quahog police officer and wheelchair-using neighbor.

gag.description: He'll out-bench-press the driver. Accessible van required; ramp must actually work.

figures.name: Bonnie Swanson
figures.realBlurb: Joe's wife, perpetually (and eternally) pregnant.

figures.name: Cleveland Brown
figures.realBlurb: Mild-mannered Griffin friend and deli owner.

figures.name: Mayor Adam West
figures.realBlurb: Quahog's gloriously unhinged mayor.

gag.description: He's at war with the number six and a squirrel he believes is a Soviet spy. Humor him; he signs the budget.

figures.name: Tom Tucker
figures.realBlurb: Vain Quahog Channel 5 news anchor.

figures.name: Tricia Takanawa
figures.realBlurb: Channel 5's deadpan field reporter.

figures.name: Ollie Williams
figures.realBlurb: Channel 5's extremely concise weatherman.

gag.description: Ask him the forecast. He'll say 'IT'S GONNA RAIN!' That's the whole ride.

figures.name: Dr. Elmer Hartman
figures.realBlurb: Quahog's spectacularly incompetent physician.

figures.name: Mort Goldman
figures.realBlurb: Anxious Quahog pharmacist.

figures.name: Carter Pewterschmidt
figures.realBlurb: Lois's tycoon father and Quahog's richest man.

figures.name: Seamus Levine
figures.realBlurb: Quahog's salty wooden-limbed sailor.

gag.description: He is mostly wood. Keep the cigarette lighter away from him. Arrr.

figures.name: Consuela
figures.realBlurb: Quahog's immovable housekeeper. 'No, no.'

figures.name: Herbert
figures.realBlurb: The Griffins' creaky-voiced elderly neighbor.

figures.name: Herbert West
figures.realBlurb: Lovecraft's 'Re-Animator,' a Miskatonic medical man with a reagent problem.

gag.description: Cargo is 'medical supplies' that should not glow. If the cargo knocks, do not open it. Keep the engine running.

figures.name: Charles Dexter Ward
figures.realBlurb: Providence antiquarian who dug too deep into his ancestor Curwen.

figures.name: Joseph Curwen
figures.realBlurb: Pawtuxet's unnervingly long-lived colonial alchemist.

gag.destination: the Curwen farm (the one with the smell)
gag.description: He looks great for 250. Don't read the Latin on the cellar door. Don't go in the cellar. Don't, actually.

figures.name: Randolph Carter
figures.realBlurb: Lovecraft's recurring dreamer-adventurer.

figures.name: Richard Upton Pickman
figures.realBlurb: Boston-Arkham painter with disquietingly accurate models.

figures.name: Henry Armitage
figures.realBlurb: Miskatonic University librarian and keeper of the Necronomicon.

gag.destination: the Miskatonic University Library, restricted stacks
gag.description: Returning one (1) very old book. Do not read aloud from it at the stop sign. Late fees are the least of it.

figures.name: Wilbur Whateley
figures.realBlurb: The unsettlingly tall Dunwich prodigy with a secret in the attic.

gag.description: Needs the big van; he's nine feet tall and growing. The twin brother stays home. Trust us, the twin stays home.

figures.name: Lavinia Whateley
figures.realBlurb: Albino Dunwich matriarch of the Whateley line.

figures.name: Obed Marsh
figures.realBlurb: Innsmouth sea captain who struck a very bad bargain with the deep.

gag.description: Bring the gold. Don't comment on the 'Innsmouth look.' Be home before the tide.

figures.name: Asenath Waite
figures.realBlurb: Innsmouth-born woman of unusually forceful... personality.

figures.name: Robert Olmstead
figures.realBlurb: Traveler who took the cheap bus into Innsmouth and regretted it.

figures.name: Zadok Allen
figures.realBlurb: Innsmouth's 96-year-old town drunk and unreliable oracle.

gag.description: Buy him a bottle and he'll tell you EVERYTHING about 1846. Maybe more than you want. Iä.

figures.name: Keziah Mason
figures.realBlurb: Arkham's witch of 'The Dreams in the Witch House' and odd geometry.

figures.name: Walter Gilman
figures.realBlurb: Miskatonic math student who rented the wrong angled room.

figures.name: Thomas F. Malone
figures.realBlurb: Detective of 'The Horror at Red Hook.'

figures.name: Crawford Tillinghast
figures.realBlurb: Inventor of the 'From Beyond' resonator. Probably shouldn't have.

figures.name: Erich Zann
figures.realBlurb: Mute violist whose late-night music kept something at bay.

figures.name: Edward Pickman Derby
figures.realBlurb: Arkham poet who married very, very unwisely.

figures.name: Carolyn Perron
figures.realBlurb: Mother whose 1971 Harrisville farmhouse inspired 'The Conjuring.'

gag.description: The clocks all stop at 3:07 a.m. The dog won't go inside. Please hurry, and bring a priest if you've got one.

figures.name: Roger Perron
figures.realBlurb: Father of the Harrisville family at the center of the Conjuring case.

figures.name: Bathsheba Sherman
figures.realBlurb: 19th-c. Harrisville woman of grim local legend.

figures.name: Ed Warren
figures.realBlurb: Self-styled demonologist of the Conjuring cases.

figures.name: Lorraine Warren
figures.realBlurb: Self-described clairvoyant and paranormal investigator.

gag.description: Do not touch anything in the back room. Especially not the doll. Genuinely, leave the doll alone.

figures.name: Little Bett
figures.realBlurb: Touring elephant shot crossing a Chepachet bridge in 1826.

gag.description: One elephant. Route AROUND the grist-mill bridge; the locals get twitchy and armed. Peanuts in the glovebox.

figures.name: Peleg Walker
figures.realBlurb: Ramtail Factory watchman whose ghost still rings the mill bell.

gag.description: He works the night shift. He has worked it since 1822. The bell rings itself; ignore it.

figures.name: Michael Townsend
figures.realBlurb: Artist who secretly built an apartment inside the Providence Place Mall.

gag.destination: a 'forgotten' corner of Providence Place Mall
gag.description: 5,000 lbs of cinderblock and one futon, in through the parking garage. Tell no one. It's art.

figures.name: Rocky Marciano
figures.realBlurb: Undefeated heavyweight champ from Brockton, just up the line.

figures.name: Ruth Buzzi
figures.realBlurb: Wequetequock-raised 'Laugh-In' comedian with a mean purse.

figures.name: Viola Davis
figures.realBlurb: EGOT-winning actress raised in Central Falls, RI.

figures.name: James Woods
figures.realBlurb: Warwick-raised actor; recurring Family Guy guest as himself.

figures.name: Debra Messing
figures.realBlurb: Actress who grew up in East Greenwich, RI.

figures.name: The Big Nazo Creatures
figures.realBlurb: Providence's roving troupe of giant foam alien puppets.

gag.description: Eight foam aliens in the back. They will honk at pedestrians. Roll the windows down; they need air holes.

figures.name: H. P. Tillinghast
figures.realBlurb: Old Pawtuxet Valley mill family name still on the road signs.

figures.name: Sarah Tirrell
figures.realBlurb: Foster farmwife tied to the Ramtail haunting lore.

figures.name: Captain William Mayes
figures.realBlurb: Pirate who owned Newport's White Horse Tavern, still standing.

gag.description: Oldest tavern in America; he should know, he owned it. Cash only, preferably Spanish pieces of eight.

figures.name: Thomas Paine
figures.realBlurb: Buccaneer-turned-Newport-pillar who helped build Trinity Church.

figures.name: Samuel Cranston
figures.realBlurb: RI's 30-term governor, once held captive seven years by pirates.

gag.description: Was held by pirates for seven years; came home the night his wife was to remarry. Watch the docks. Always watch the docks.

figures.name: Ned Low
figures.realBlurb: Sadistic pirate whose crew was hanged en masse at Newport in 1723.

figures.name: George M. Cohan Jr.
figures.realBlurb: Heir to the Providence showman's name and grin.

figures.name: Christopher Lloyd
figures.realBlurb: Actor (Doc Brown) raised partly in New England; great-time-machine energy.

figures.name: Wendy Lawton
figures.realBlurb: Foster-Glocester storyteller of the Ancients & Horribles tradition.

figures.name: Cynthia Wilcox
figures.realBlurb: Chepachet shopkeeper whose family ran the village store for generations.

figures.name: Betsey Williams
figures.realBlurb: Descendant of Roger Williams who gave her farm to Roger Williams Park.

figures.name: Doctor Jekyll Munoz
figures.realBlurb: Lovecraft's Dr. Munoz of 'Cool Air,' who must be kept very, very cold.

gag.description: Crank the A/C to maximum. If it fails, do NOT open the door. Things go badly above 55.

figures.name: Robert Suydam
figures.realBlurb: Reclusive scholar of forbidden lore from 'The Horror at Red Hook.'

figures.name: Barnabas Marsh
figures.realBlurb: Latter-day heir to the Innsmouth Marsh line and refinery.

figures.name: Eliza Orne
figures.realBlurb: Old Innsmouth-Arkham family name whispered in the histories.

figures.name: Abigail Prinn
figures.realBlurb: Witch of pulp-horror Arkham, executed but not entirely gone.

figures.name: Nahum Gardner
figures.realBlurb: Farmer of 'The Colour Out of Space,' whose well went wrong.

gag.description: Bring a sample of the well water and the gray, crumbling vegetables. Wear gloves. Maybe don't drink the coffee he offers.

figures.name: Ammi Pierce
figures.realBlurb: Dunwich neighbor and witness to the blasted heath.

figures.name: Daniel Upton
figures.realBlurb: Friend and biographer of the ill-fated Edward Derby.

figures.name: Frank Pabodie
figures.realBlurb: Miskatonic engineer who built the drill for the Antarctic expedition.

figures.name: William Dyer (the geologist)
figures.realBlurb: Miskatonic geologist-narrator of 'At the Mountains of Madness.'

figures.name: Edith Wharton
figures.realBlurb: Novelist who summered at Land's End in Newport and skewered its society.

gag.description: Will take notes on everyone for the next novel. Assume you are a character. You are.

figures.name: Henry James
figures.realBlurb: Novelist who spent formative years in 1860s Newport.

figures.name: Julia Ward Howe
figures.realBlurb: Wrote 'Battle Hymn of the Republic'; summered at Oak Glen in Portsmouth.

gag.description: Mine eyes have seen the glory. Also the parking situation. Bring the suffrage pamphlets.

figures.name: Maud Howe Elliott
figures.realBlurb: Pulitzer-winning author and Newport civic force; daughter of Julia Ward Howe.

figures.name: S. J. Perelman
figures.realBlurb: Humorist and Marx Brothers screenwriter who grew up in Providence.

gag.description: Needs a new ribbon and a fresh supply of withering adjectives. Brown class of '25.

figures.name: Edwin O'Connor
figures.realBlurb: Providence novelist of 'The Last Hurrah,' the great machine-politics tale.

figures.name: Ann Hood
figures.realBlurb: West Warwick-born novelist and memoirist.

figures.name: Jhumpa Lahiri
figures.realBlurb: Pulitzer-winning author raised in Kingston, RI.

figures.name: Bill Cowsill
figures.realBlurb: Eldest of the Newport family band that inspired 'The Partridge Family.'

gag.description: The whole family is the band. Mom's in the band. The bus seats nine. 'Hair' on repeat.

figures.name: Susan Cowsill
figures.realBlurb: Youngest Cowsill sibling and a working musician to this day.

figures.name: Barbara 'Mini-Mom' Cowsill
figures.realBlurb: The mother who actually sang in the Cowsills.

figures.name: Kristin Hersh
figures.realBlurb: Throwing Muses leader, born in Atlanta but a Newport/Aquidneck product.

figures.name: Tanya Donelly
figures.realBlurb: Belly and Throwing Muses singer with deep Newport roots.

figures.name: John McCauley
figures.realBlurb: Deer Tick frontman from the Providence scene.

figures.name: Scott Hamilton
figures.realBlurb: Providence-born mainstream jazz saxophonist.

figures.name: Jerry Harrison
figures.realBlurb: Talking Heads keyboardist/guitarist (Modern Lovers alum).

figures.name: Meredith Vieira
figures.realBlurb: Providence-born TV journalist and game-show host.

figures.name: Eddie Dowling
figures.realBlurb: Woonsocket-born Pulitzer-winning Broadway producer and actor.

figures.name: Theodore Foster
figures.realBlurb: Early U.S. senator; the town of Foster, RI is named for him.

figures.name: Jabez Bowen
figures.realBlurb: Revolutionary-era deputy governor and Brown benefactor.

figures.name: Christopher Champlin
figures.realBlurb: Newport merchant prince of the colonial Atlantic trade.

figures.name: Caleb Carr
figures.realBlurb: Colonial governor who died in office in 1695.

figures.name: Walter Clarke
figures.realBlurb: Quaker governor who hid the colony's charter from Governor Andros.

gag.description: Carrying one (1) Royal Charter. If Governor Andros asks, you've never seen it. He won't take an oath, so don't make him.

figures.name: John Coggeshall
figures.realBlurb: First President of the Colony, 1647; voted to acquit Anne Hutchinson.

figures.name: Nicholas Cooke
figures.realBlurb: Revolutionary war governor who kept RI in the fight.

figures.name: William Greene
figures.realBlurb: Revolutionary-era governor of Rhode Island.

figures.name: Job Almy
figures.realBlurb: Portsmouth Quaker farmer of the old Aquidneck families.

figures.name: Princess Red Wing
figures.realBlurb: Narragansett-Wampanoag historian and keeper of the Tomaquag museum.

figures.name: Ella Sekatau
figures.realBlurb: Narragansett ethnohistorian and medicine woman.

figures.name: Canonchet
figures.realBlurb: Narragansett war leader of King Philip's War ('I shall die before my heart is soft').

figures.name: Quaiapen
figures.realBlurb: The only female sachem of King Philip's War; built Queen's Fort.

figures.name: Clem Labine
figures.realBlurb: Woonsocket-born Brooklyn Dodgers relief ace.

figures.name: Chris Terreri
figures.realBlurb: Providence-born NHL goaltender and Stanley Cup winner.

figures.name: Brian Lawton
figures.realBlurb: Cumberland, RI native; first U.S.-born No. 1 NHL draft pick.

figures.name: Will Blackmon
figures.realBlurb: Providence-raised NFL defensive back.

figures.name: Len Wilkens
figures.realBlurb: Hall of Fame coach who led the Providence Reds-era hardwood faithful.

figures.name: Abdul Alhazred
figures.realBlurb: Lovecraft's 'Mad Arab,' fictional author of the Necronomicon.

gag.description: Selling one (1) heavily annotated manuscript. Do not let him read from it at the light. Do not, in fact, make eye contact.

figures.name: Francis Wayland Thurston
figures.realBlurb: Providence narrator who pieced together 'The Call of Cthulhu.'

figures.name: George Gammell Angell
figures.realBlurb: Brown professor of Semitic languages and keeper of the Cthulhu file.

figures.name: Inspector Legrasse
figures.realBlurb: Detective who raided the Cthulhu cult in the bayou.

figures.name: Nathaniel Wingate Peaslee
figures.realBlurb: Miskatonic professor of 'The Shadow Out of Time' whose mind went traveling.

gag.description: Lost five years and came back writing in a script that predates humanity. Be patient; he's catching up on current events.

figures.name: Andrea Perron
figures.realBlurb: Eldest Perron daughter who wrote the books behind 'The Conjuring.'

figures.name: Cindy Perron
figures.realBlurb: Perron sister of the Harrisville farmhouse haunting.

figures.name: Daisy Bruguière
figures.realBlurb: Late Gilded-Age Newport hostess of the Wakehurst estate.

figures.name: Eldridge Snow
figures.realBlurb: Old Pawtuxet Valley name behind the Autocrat coffee-syrup trade.

gag.description: Two jugs of coffee syrup; it's the official state drink and he will quiz you on the ratio. Two tablespoons per glass.

figures.name: Stuyvesant Fish
figures.realBlurb: Railroad president whose firing was hastened by his wife Mamie's social snubs.

figures.name: Frederick W. Vanderbilt
figures.realBlurb: Banjo-playing Vanderbilt who built Rough Point, then fled Newport's social grind.

figures.name: James Van Alen
figures.realBlurb: Anglophile 'American Prince of Wales' who left the U.S. over Prohibition.

gag.serviceName: One-way ride to the steamship pier
gag.description: Relocating permanently to France; democracy has become inconvenient and the bar has closed. Steamer trunks only.

figures.name: Prudence Slater-Whipple
figures.realBlurb: Invented great-great-granddaughter of Samuel Slater; keeps a spindle from the old Pawtucket mill on her mantel.

figures.name: Obadiah Slater
figures.realBlurb: Invented Slater-mill descendant; still calls Sunday dinner "the second shift."

figures.name: Mercy Williams-Tefft
figures.realBlurb: Invented descendant of Roger Williams; hosts a soul-liberty book club that argues about everything.

figures.name: Josiah Williams
figures.realBlurb: Invented Williams-line descendant; paddles the Seekonk every June to honor the banishment route.

figures.name: Faith Hutchinson-Cole
figures.realBlurb: Invented descendant of Anne Hutchinson; famously wins every zoning-board argument.

figures.name: Ezra Hopkins
figures.realBlurb: Invented descendant of Stephen Hopkins; signs every birthday card with a deliberately shaky hand.

figures.name: Abigail Hopkins-Brown
figures.realBlurb: Invented Hopkins descendant; runs the church rummage sale like a merchant fleet.

figures.name: Silas Greene
figures.realBlurb: Invented descendant of Nathanael Greene; re-enacts quartermaster logistics with casserole deliveries.

figures.name: Hannah Greene-Almy
figures.realBlurb: Invented Greene descendant; grows the biggest rhubarb in Kent County, allegedly.

figures.name: Caleb Perry
figures.realBlurb: Invented descendant of Oliver Hazard Perry; has not given up the ship, or the 1987 Buick.

figures.name: Louisa Perry-Chace
figures.realBlurb: Invented Perry descendant; narrates harbor tours whether or not anyone asked.

figures.name: Bertram Burnside
figures.realBlurb: Invented descendant of Ambrose Burnside; the sideburns are, tragically, hereditary.

figures.name: Cornelia Dorr-Angell
figures.realBlurb: Invented descendant of Thomas Wilson Dorr; still collects signatures for something every spring.

figures.name: Theodora Dyer
figures.realBlurb: Invented Dyer-family descendant; keeps the governors’ portraits dusted at the historical society.

figures.name: Winifred Lewis
figures.realBlurb: Invented descendant of lighthouse keeper Ida Lewis; rows to Rose Island for exercise, weather be damned.

figures.name: Augustus Stuart
figures.realBlurb: Invented descendant of Gilbert Stuart; paints dollar-bill George on request at village fundraisers.

figures.name: Rosalind Jones-Carter
figures.realBlurb: Invented descendant of soprano Sissieretta Jones; leads the senior chorus with terrifying precision.

figures.name: Percival Whipple
figures.realBlurb: Invented Whipple-family descendant; claims the family tavern once out-poured all of Providence.

figures.name: Delia Sprague
figures.realBlurb: Invented descendant of the Sprague mill dynasty; knits like the A&W looms are still running.

figures.name: Horace Arnold
figures.realBlurb: Invented descendant of colonial governor Benedict Arnold (the grandfather, he insists, not the traitor).

figures.name: Ezekiel Whipple
figures.realBlurb: Invented Whipple-family descendant; insists the tavern ledger balances if you squint.

figures.name: Clara Burnside
figures.realBlurb: Invented Burnside descendant; mercifully spared the sideburns, keeps the portrait dusted.

figures.name: Marion Sprague-Tefft
figures.realBlurb: Invented descendant of the Sprague mill dynasty; hums at loom speed when concentrating.

loginPersonas.name: Roger Williams
loginPersonas.rationale: The literal founder of the place — perfect as the village 'owner'/proprietor. Tolerant by design, lets everyone in.

loginPersonas.name: Theodore Francis Green
loginPersonas.rationale: Longest-serving elder statesman; a believable senior owner/proprietor figure who's seen every administration come and go.

loginPersonas.name: Stephen Hopkins
loginPersonas.rationale: Ten-term governor and signer — a steady hand for managing members and volunteers. 'My hand trembles, but my heart does not.'

loginPersonas.name: Buddy Cianci
loginPersonas.rationale: Quintessential city manager/operator who knew everyone and everything — great for a 'manage' role. (Keep him away from billing.)

loginPersonas.name: Elizabeth Buffum Chace
loginPersonas.rationale: Organizer and 'conscience of Rhode Island' — a trustworthy full-access coordinator who actually moved people from A to B.

loginPersonas.name: Ida Lewis
loginPersonas.rationale: Dependable, on-call rescuer — the model volunteer-coordinator with full request access. Always answers the late-night call.

loginPersonas.name: Nathanael Greene
loginPersonas.rationale: Washington's logistics-and-strategy general — a natural operations coordinator with full access to dispatch volunteers.

loginPersonas.name: Lincoln Chafee
loginPersonas.rationale: Earnest multi-office public servant; a fine general-purpose coordinator. May insist all distances be logged in kilometers.

loginPersonas.name: Claiborne Pell
loginPersonas.rationale: Genteel grant-giver — good for a benefits/aid coordinator who hands out resources to members in need.

loginPersonas.name: John O. Pastore
loginPersonas.rationale: Gruff-but-fair committee chair energy; solid for moderating members and approving requests.

loginPersonas.name: Moses Brown
loginPersonas.rationale: Principled benefactor and institution-builder — believable as a manager of programs and donations.

loginPersonas.name: Sarah Doyle
loginPersonas.rationale: Educator and organizer; a natural fit for managing onboarding and member education.

loginPersonas.name: Lincoln (Esek) Hopkins
loginPersonas.rationale: Fleet commander who got court-martialed — fun as a 'restricted' account whose privileges were dialed back after an incident.

loginPersonas.name: Thomas Wilson Dorr
loginPersonas.rationale: The rebel whose cannons misfired and who was convicted of treason — a thematically perfect 'restricted/limited' demo account.

loginPersonas.name: Abraham Whipple
loginPersonas.rationale: Burned a British ship — useful as a restricted account that can request rides but not approve anyone else's. Wanted by the Crown.
